title: betula-alnus rubra
intro: Betula-alnus rubra, also known as Red Alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. The health benefits of Red Alder include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for colds and flu. Red Alder can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in herbal medicine.
